elements.title: Elinor Barker
elements.contents: ... that Elinor Barker (pictured) won an Olympic medal while pregnant?

elements.title: a 2025 adventure film
elements.contents: ... that a tram chase sequence in a 2025 adventure film was shot in two different theme parks, and with two different trams?

elements.title: Miroslava Pešíková
elements.contents: ... that Miroslava Pešíková was a ballerina at the National Theatre in Prague for 28 years in 47 roles?

elements.title: first session of the United Nations General Assembly
elements.contents: ... that the first session of the United Nations General Assembly was held in a church in war-damaged London only four months after the end of World War II?

elements.title: Joan Salter
elements.contents: ... that the Holocaust survivor Joan Salter returned from America to parents she could not remember?

elements.title: Seven Samurai
elements.contents: ... that Seven Samurai is considered one of the greatest films ever made?

elements.title: Sadiku
elements.contents: ... that despite its hardline Islamic beliefs, a Boko Haram faction led by Sadiku tolerated allied bandits' use of alcohol, drugs, and prostitution?

elements.title: Special Engineer Detachment
elements.contents: ... that John D. Hoffman of the Special Engineer Detachment was awarded the Soldier's Medal, the US Army's highest non-combat decoration and the only one given to a member of the Manhattan District?

elements.title: Mark Williams
elements.contents: ... that Mark Williams did his press conference naked after winning the 2018 World Snooker Championship?
